compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Fix in PR #69 (branch fix/janitor-tickets-gc, off origin/main): _is_entry strips the 'tickets-' prefix before the hex check so tickets-<sha> entries are GC'd + byte-counted like code <sha> entries. RED->GREEN regression test (test_evicts_stale_pinned_ticket_store_entry) + mutation-checked; 17 janitor tests pass; lint+typecheck green. Ready to close once #69 merges.

compiled_state.description:
The snapshot janitor never garbage-collects the new pinned ticket-store cache entries (`<store>/tickets-<sha>/`) introduced in PR #67, so they accumulate unboundedly as the `tickets` branch changes — a local-disk leak in the gate snapshot cache. They are also absent from the janitor's byte-total accounting.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Run the plan-review / completion gates repeatedly while the `tickets` branch HEAD changes between runs (normal: every ticket write advances it).
2. Each distinct tickets SHA materializes a fresh `<store>/tickets-<sha>/.tickets-tracker/` copy of the whole ticket store.
3. Inspect the snapshot store (`REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR` / `store_root()`): the `tickets-<sha>` dirs accumulate and are never evicted.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: the janitor evicts stale `tickets-<sha>` entries by the same grace/LRU/size policy it applies to code-snapshot `<sha>` entries, and counts them in the byte total.
- Actual: `src/rebar/_snapshot/janitor.py:_is_entry` treats an entry as `all(c in "0123456789abcdef" for c in name)` — the `tickets-` prefix is non-hex, so `_is_entry("tickets-<sha>")` is False. `_entries()` therefore excludes them: they are never evicted (leak) and never counted (under-reported cache size).

## Root Cause
`_is_entry` (janitor.py) recognizes only all-hex directory names; PR #67's `tickets-<sha>` entries don't match.

## Fix direction
Extend `_is_entry` to also recognize `tickets-<40hex>` (e.g. accept names matching `^(tickets-)?[0-9a-f]{40}$`), so `_entries()` includes them for eviction + accounting. Verify `_evict`/`_remove_sidecars` behave for a `tickets-`-named entry (no caveats sidecar — the unlink is try/except, harmless). Add a janitor test that a stale `tickets-<sha>` entry is evicted past the grace window.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_is_entry` returns True for a `tickets-<40hex>` dir; `_entries()` includes it.
- [ ] The janitor evicts a stale `tickets-<sha>` entry (past grace) and counts it in the byte total.
- [ ] A regression test covers eviction + accounting of a `tickets-<sha>` entry.
- [ ] Code-snapshot `<sha>` entry handling is unchanged.
- [ ] Fix lands via PR to main.
compiled_state.title: snapshot janitor leaks pinned ticket-store entries (tickets-<sha> not GC'd)
